title: Lippia globiflora
taxonomy: Lippia globiflora is a species of flowering plant in the verbena family, Verbenaceae. It is native to Brazil and is commonly found in the savannas and grasslands of the country. Lippia globiflora is a member of the genus Lippia, which consists of over 150 species of plants, most of which are found in the Americas. The genus Lippia is part of the larger clade Asterids, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the mint family, Lamiaceae. Lippia globiflora is closely related to other plants in the Verbenaceae family, such as the verbena and the lantana.
uses: Lippia globiflora is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in South Africa. The plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds and skin conditions. Its leaves and stems are often used to make teas and infusions that are said to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. Additionally, Lippia globiflora has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Lippia globiflora.

preparations: Lippia globiflora is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
